concepts.title: Transformation of prodcut into sum or differences
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformation of product into sum or differences

  • $2\text{sinAcosB}=\text{sin}\left( \text{A}+\text{B} \right)+\text{sin}\left( \text{A}-\text{B} \right)$
  • $2\text{cos}A\text{sin}B=\text{sin}\left( A+B \right)-\text{sin}\left( A-B \right)$
  • $2\text{cosAcosB}=\text{cos}\left( \text{A}+\text{B} \right)+\text{cos}\left( \text{A}-\text{B} \right)$
  • $2\text{sinAsinB}=\text{cos}\left( \text{A}-\text{B} \right)-\text{cos}\left( \text{A}+\text{B} \right)$

concepts.title: Transformation of sum and differences into product
concepts.conceptBody:
Transformation of sum & differences into product

  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ sinA}+\text{sinB}=2\text{sin}\left( \frac{\text{A}+\text{B}}{2} \right)\text{cos}\left( \frac{\text{A}-\text{B}}{2} \right)$
  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ sinA}-\text{sinB}=2\text{sin}\left( \frac{\text{A}-\text{B}}{2} \right)\text{cos}\left( \frac{\text{A}+\text{B}}{2} \right)$
  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ cosA}+\text{cosB}=2\text{cos}\left( \frac{\text{A}+\text{B}}{2} \right)\text{cos}\left( \frac{\text{A}-\text{B}}{2} \right)$
  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ cosA}-\text{cosB}=-2\text{sin}\left( \frac{\text{A}+\text{B}}{2} \right)\text{sin}\left( \frac{\text{A}-\text{B}}{2} \right)$

examples.title: If $\cos x+\cos y+\cos \alpha=0$ and $\sin x+\sin y+\sin \alpha=0$, then $\cot \left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right)=$
